headline: The Retiree Who Chose $65,000 Instead of $120,000 and Ended Up Richer
summary: Two retirees walk into retirement with the same amount of money. One builds a portfolio that pays $120,000 a year but barely grows. The other settles for $65,000 a year and focuses on dividend growth. At first glance, the first retiree appears to have won. Twenty-five years later, the second retiree may have collected more ... The Retiree Who Chose $65,000 Instead of $120,000 and Ended Up Richer

headline: 64 Years of Raises: How DGRO Finds Companies That Never Cut Dividends
summary: The iShares Core Dividend Growth ETF (NYSEARCA:DGRO) was built for investors who want a paycheck that gets bigger every year, not a yield chase. DGRO tracks the Morningstar US Dividend Growth Index, screening for companies with at least five consecutive years of dividend growth and payout ratios under 75%. The fund pays a roughly 2.2% ... 64 Years of Raises: How DGRO Finds Companies That Never Cut Dividends

headline: This $1.7 Million Portfolio Pays More Than a Member of Congress
summary: A rank-and-file member of Congress earns $174,000 per year. A dividend portfolio can generate the same level of income without a campaign, constituents, or a weekly commute to Washington. Unlike a salary, however, this income is tied to capital, which means the size of the portfolio matters far more than the title attached to the ... This $1.7 Million Portfolio Pays More Than a Member of Congress

headline: Today’s AI market hits all 5 warning signs that preceded 2000 Nasdaq crash.
summary: Although Wall Street has spent the better part of two years cheering an artificial intelligence buildout that has dragged the Nasdaq 100 up roughly 30% over the past year, the technology-packed Nasdaq Composite is starting to wobble at the worst possible moment. The benchmark S&P 500 has slipped, with SPY off about 4% in the ... Today’s AI market hits all 5 warning signs that preceded 2000 Nasdaq crash.

headline: Johnson & Johnson Invests more than $1 Billion to Strengthen U.S. Vision Manufacturing in Jacksonville, Florida
summary: NEW BRUNSWICK, N.J., June 15, 2026--Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) (the "Company"), healthcare’s leading, most comprehensive innovation powerhouse, today announced an investment of more than $1 billion in Jacksonville, Florida to strengthen its Vision operations by scaling U.S.-based manufacturing, packaging and distribution capabilities.

headline: Reported Saturday, Johnson & Johnson's TALVEY Plus DARZALEX FASPRO Delivers Up To 81% PFS And 89% OS At 24 Months In First Phase 3 Study To Show Superior PFS For GPRC5D Bispecific Combination In Multiple Myeloma
summary: TALVEY plus DARZALEX FASPRO with or without pomalidomide showed progression-free survival of up to 81% and overall survival of up to 89% at 24 monthsMonumenTAL-3 is the third positive study in recent months
